title: Charred Chewy Uruguayan Kinako Cobbler
description: A unique kinako-based dessert with a crispy, charred exterior and chewy interior, perfect for a weekend brunch. Inspired by Uruguayan flavors and traditions.

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.notes: Dust the kinako with cornstarch for a lighter texture.

instructions: Preheat the oven to 40092 F (20092 C).
Mix the kinako, brown sugar, and cornstarch in a bowl until well combined.
Heat the water and coconut oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the salt, kinako extract, and vanilla extract to the kinako mixture and stir well.
Brush the kinako mixture onto the inside of a baking dish coated with coconut oil.
Pour the nimono-simmer (water mixture) into the baking dish and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes or until the kinako absorbs the liquid.
Let the kinako cool and soak in apple cider vinegar for at least 30 minutes.
Transfer the kinako to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown.
Let the cobbler cool before serving.
Serve warm or at room temperature with your choice of toppings.
Garnish with fresh kinako or kinako-based desserts if desired.
